pages.desc: A sak-yant tattoo stencil captioned in modern Thai ยันต์ดอกบัวทอง (yant dok bua thong, the golden-lotus yant). A single tall vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script runs the length of the leaf like

A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์สาริกาคู่ (yant sarika khu, the paired-sarika yant). Two facing sarika (myna) birds perch head-to-head atop a long vertical column of Tham Lanna spell-script, three u

Two sak-yant stencils side by side. Left, captioned ยันต์ ๕ แถว (yant ha thaew, the five-row yant): five parallel vertical lines of dense Tham Lanna script, each capped by an unalom spike — the five-l

A sak-yant sheet of two money-bag roundels, captioned ยันต์ถุงเงินถุงทอง … โภคทรัพย์ (yant thung ngoen thung thong, the silver-bag gold-bag yant; phokha sap, wealth). Each is a drawn pouch: a quartere

Sak-yant tracing stencil. Two "yant dok bua" (lotus yantras) drawn one above the other in fine black outline; caption below reads ๑ ยันต์ดอกบัว ฯ ("No. 1, Lotus Yantra"). Each yant is a stylised lotus

Sak-yant tracing stencil. A single composite yant: a central lotus with a flame/unalome finial and three spire-markers above (the middle one a rounded uposatha/spade marker), flanked left and right by

Sak-yant tracing stencil, a second "yant sarika khu" (paired-sarika) variant. A central lotus topped by a flame/unalome finial with three spire-markers rises between two facing sarika birds; a central

A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์นกยูง (yant nok yung, the peacock yant). Two peacocks with long sweeping tail-plumes stand facing, flanking a central tied money-bag/heart, the whole enclosed in a br

A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์หงส์คำ (yant hong kham, the golden-hamsa yant). A single hamsa (celestial swan/goose) rises with wings spread and a long curling scrolled tail, wrapped in an arc of T

A sak-yant stencil captioned ยันต์พญาไก่ฟ้า (yant phaya kai fa, the lord-pheasant / firebird yant). A crested pheasant-phoenix sweeps across the leaf with a long trailing plumed tail, a small Na-circl

A sak-yant sheet of two butterfly stencils, captioned ยันต์ผีเสื้อ (yant phi suea, the butterfly yant). Above, a symmetrical open-winged butterfly with patterned wings, an unalom crown and an arc of T

A sak-yant stencil captioned as a Hanuman yant. Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaps in three-quarter view, brandishing a staff/trident and a pennoned flag, mouth open, tail curling; his limbs and t

A sak-yant sheet of two ruesi (lersi) heads, captioned ยันต์เศียรฤๅษี พ่อแก่ (yant sian ruesi, pho kae — the head of the hermit-teacher, the Old Father). Above, a frontal face of the ascetic master cr
A finely inked lotus blossom standing alone at the centre of an otherwise blank leaf: a full flower drawn in fine hatching, layered pointed petals opening around a beaded seed-pod crown, with a small

A small decorative vignette centred on a blank leaf: a symmetrical lotus flower rising from a paired base, flanked left and right by two fish (or fish-tailed birds) with feathery plumed sprays curling

A decorative sak-yant vignette of two facing birds, drawn mirror-image with scaled bodies, upright crests and long dripping plumed tails that curl toward each other. No caption or spell-script — a pai

A sampler leaf of six small charm motifs for tracing, ungrouped: top-left a Ganesha head crowned with an Om glyph (remover of obstacles, success); top-right a rearing naga serpent (water-lord, protect
Two blank money-bag templates for tracing, one above the other: the upper a round pouch with a lotus-frilled neck, its face ruled into an empty quartered grid ready to take a four-syllable Na-square;
Two gourd motifs: above, a small bottle-gourd (namtao) tied at the neck; below, a larger gourd with a tokay gecko (tukkae) climbing it, splay-toed feet gripping the swell, tail curling into a spiral.

Gecko charm stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) with star-splayed toes flanking a central lotus blossom, tails curling outward; below, a single gecko poised over a looped heart-shape. The tukka
More gecko stencils: above, two tokay geckos (tukkae) crossing tails in a symmetrical mirror, star-toed feet spread; below, a single gecko reaching toward a small round fruit or gourd it holds in its

Two lotus stencils in fine-line contemporary style, one above the other: the upper a pointed-petal lotus half-opened around a sharp central bud, the lower a rounder full-bloom lotus with a heart-shape

Two lotus motifs, finely hatched: above, an ornate frontal lotus rosette opened flat like a mandala around a beaded seed-crown; below, a closed lotus bud on a long curling stem that resolves into a sm

A symmetrical fine-line composition: a central lotus blossom flanked left and right by two ornate birds with long feathered sweeping plumes curling upward and back. No caption or script — a decorative

A fine-line stencil: a full lotus blossom above, with two peacocks below facing inward, their long feathered tail-sprays fanning up and outward on either side. No script or caption — a decorative peac

A fine-line stencil: a pointed-petal lotus at centre with two swallows/birds in flight below it, wings drawn as sweeping feathered plumes curling outward. No caption or spell-script — a decorative bir

A fine-line stencil: two peacocks facing inward flank a small round money-bag tied at the neck, their long ornate tail-feathers sweeping down and out in mirrored arcs. No caption or script — a decorat

Two fine-line bird stencils: above, two long-tailed birds facing outward with scrolled plumed tails sweeping apart in mirror; below, two birds flanking a small bottle-gourd (namtao), their tails cross

Two fine-line stencils: above, two parrots/lovebirds set head-to-head so their bodies and folded wings form a heart (a love/coupling motif); below, a plump owl perched on a round orb or fruit, feather

A single fine-line peacock standing with its tail fanned upright behind it in a spray of eyed plumes, body finely scaled, small crest on the head. No caption or spell-script — a decorative peacock ste

Two fine-line stencils of Thai mythical serpent-birds (naga- or makara-headed hong in the kranok idiom), each drawn in flight with a crested serpentine head, scaled body and flame-scrolled tail. No ca

A single fine-line phoenix/hong rising with wings spread wide and a long tail resolving into a cascade of ink-scroll spirals below. Small crest and open beak. No caption or spell-script — decorative f
An ornate fine-line firebird/phoenix in profile, wings and body finely scaled, with a great plumed peacock-eyed tail sweeping up and back in flame-like fronds. No caption or script — decorative flash
Two fine-line rooster stencils, one above the other: a proud cockerel (kai) with arched sickle tail-feathers and finely scaled breast, and below a second cock striding with raised hackles. No caption

Two fine-line butterfly stencils: above, a symmetrical pair of open butterfly wings (drawn as a mirrored pair, split at the body); below, a single butterfly banked to one side with trailing scrolled l

Fish stencils in fine line: above, two round-bodied fish (gourami/pla) facing nose-to-nose with ruffled fins; below, a single koi-style carp swimming upward, body finely scaled. No caption or spell-sc

A single fine-line leaping deer (kwang) in mid-bound, its antlers stylised into curling flame-fronds, a patterned kranok band looping around its neck and haunch. No caption or spell-script — decorativ

A figural stencil of a four-armed deity astride Garuda: Phra Narai (Vishnu) crowned with a tiered mongkut, bearing discus, conch, club and trident in his hands, mounted on the winged bird-man Khrut wh

Two figural stencils of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior in acrobatic leaping poses, one above the other, mirrored diagonally. Each wears a crown and patterned loincloth, body finely tattooed with orn

A figural stencil of a yaksha (giant) in the Ramakien idiom: a crowned, fanged demon-warrior striding with a long sword raised overhead in one hand and a bow held in the other, ornamented armour and s

An intricate circular figural stencil: a fanged guardian face at the centre wrapped in a churning knot of naga serpents, banners and blades radiating outward, all densely ornamented. No caption or scr

A figural stencil of Hanuman leaping, the crowned white monkey brandishing a trident in one raised hand and a pennoned banner-staff in the other, tail curling, body finely ornamented. No caption or sc

A figural stencil of a crowned deva/warrior astride a mythical lion-beast (rachasi/singha), holding aloft a long pennoned banner-flag, the mount rearing with flame-scrolled mane and tail. No caption o

A figural stencil of Hanuman the white monkey-warrior leaping, a sword raised in one hand, the other flung up, tail sweeping into an ornamented scroll beneath him, body crowned and finely patterned. N

A figural stencil of a crowned yaksha/deva warrior standing frontally on a woven knot of naga serpents, fanged face, ornamented armour and sash, a trident-blade in one hand. No caption or script — a g

A figural stencil of a crouching figure drawing a great bow that arcs into a heart-shaped loop around him; his whole body — chest, thighs, arms — is covered in spiral koh-marks like an all-over protec

A figural stencil of a striding tiger (suea) roaring, body fully striped, one forepaw raised with claws spread, tail lashing. No caption or script — the tiger is a paramount Lanna power-emblem carryin
A figural stencil of a roaring tiger head (na suea), jaws open with bared fangs and lolling tongue, whiskers and ruff drawn as flaming fronds, striped brow. No caption or script — the tiger-face is a

Two prowling-beast stencils: above, a striped tiger (suea) creeping head-down with tail trailing; below, a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) in the mural idiom, flame-scrolled mane and clawed feet.

A figural stencil of a mythical lion-beast (singha/rachasi) prowling, its dragon-like crested head thrown up, flame-scroll mane, ridged spine and clawed feet, tail curling into a flame-frond. No capti

A figural stencil of a Lanna guardian-lion (singha/rachasi) prowling in the mural idiom: crested dragon-like head with bared teeth and cross-hatched maw, flame-frond mane and ridged joints, clawed fee

Two serpent stencils: above, a single snake rising in an S-curve, its lower body looped into a heart; below, two snakes rearing head-to-head from an interlaced love-knot of their own braided bodies. N

A figural stencil of a ruesi (lersi) in profile: a bearded ascetic-sage with hooded brow and long goatee, crowned with a tall flame-tipped headdress and wearing a strand of prayer-beads across the bar
A sheet of two teacher/deity heads: above, a frontal crowned face with a tiered pointed mongkut and calm features (a thewada/deva head); below, a profile bearded ruesi (lersi) in a flame-tipped crown
